rows.name: Argument from Expert Opinion
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on expertise-relation (E is an expert in domain D). Walton's flagship scheme. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Popular Opinion
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on majority-belief relation (most people in reference group G believe P). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Popular Practice
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on common-practice relation (most people in G do A). Parent `popular_opinion` is structurally compatible (both authority_family, both non-rigid, both dependent). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Witness Testimony
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on testimony-relation (witness W reports event E). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Causal (If cause then effect)
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on causal relation. Confidence: high. Note: near-duplicate of `cause_to_effect` — Spec 4 verifier should compare.

rows.name: Inference to the Best Explanation
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on explanation relation (E best explains O among candidate explanations). Distinct from causal — abductive structure. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Composition
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on whole-part relation (parts of W have property P → W has P). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Division
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on whole-part relation (W has P → parts of W have P). Logical converse of composition. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Classification/Definition
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on classificatory category (item I has property P → I is a member of class C). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Definition to Verbal Classification
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on definition relation, then classificatory category. Parent `verbal_classification` structurally compatible. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Verbal Classification
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on linguistic classificatory category. Walton-distinct from `classification` proper (verbal vs material). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Lack of Evidence (Negative Evidence)
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on evidential-gap relation (no evidence for P after thorough search → not P). Walton's `ad ignorantiam`. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Methodological Critique (NON-STANDARD)
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on methodology relation (study S used method M; M is unreliable for question Q → S's conclusion is unreliable). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Sample to Population (Statistical Generalization)
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on sample-to-population relation. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Negative Consequences
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on consequence relation (doing X has consequence C; C is bad → X should not be done). Parent `practical_reasoning` compatible. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Positive Consequences
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on consequence relation (doing X has consequence C; C is good → X should be done). Parent `practical_reasoning` structurally compatible. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Practical Reasoning (Goal→Means→Ought)
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on goal-means relation (agent A has goal G; A doing X realises G → A should do X). Walton's master practical-reasoning scheme. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Slippery Slope
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on causal chain (X → X' → … → bad outcome). Parent `negative_consequences` compatible (three-level chain: slippery_slope → negative_consequences → practical_reasoning, all non-rigid/dependent). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Value‑based Practical Reasoning
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on value-promotion relation (doing X promotes value V; V is held by A → A should do X). Parent `practical_reasoning` compatible. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Analogy
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on similarity-relation (case C₁ and case C₂ are similar in respect R; C₁ has property P → C₂ has P). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Example
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on exemplar-relation (E is an example of P; E has feature F → P-instances have F). Confidence: high.

rows.name: Test Scheme 2
rows.violationDescription: Soft signal: independent child (`scheme_test`) under dependent parent (`analogy`) — category-mixing in the hierarchy. Not a strict OntoClean violation; recorded for the Q-014 folksonomy diagnosis.
rows.classifierNotes: Test placeholder parented under `analogy`. **Folksonomy signal — strong**: independent placeholder under dependent substantive parent is a category-mixing pattern. Should be deleted from production catalogue or moved to a `_test_fixtures` cluster. Not a strict OntoClean violation but recorded as `violation` for surfacing. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Bare Assertion
rows.classifierNotes: Dialogue-baseline scheme — speaker simply asserts P with no inferential structure. Independent (no extrinsic relation required). Not really a Walton scheme; functions as a substrate-internal dialogue placeholder. Cluster tag missing. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Causal Reasoning
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on causal relation (specifically cause→effect direction). **Folksonomy signal** — near-duplicate of `causal` with a directional refinement that may or may not be load-bearing. Cluster tag missing (null). Spec 4 verifier candidate. Confidence: high.

Dialogue-meta scheme (meta-question about a claim's clarity, not an argument-inference pattern). Independent. **Folksonomy signal** — category-mixing: meta-CQ-style entries live in the `ArgumentScheme` table alongside Walton schemes. Confidence: high.
Dialogue-meta — meta-question about relevance. Same category-mixing signal as `claim_clarity`. Confidence: high.
Dialogue-meta — meta-question about truth. Same category-mixing signal. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Expert Opinion
rows.classifierNotes: Hyphenated duplicate of `expert_opinion`. **Folksonomy signal** — catalogue contains naming-convention duplicates. Spec 4 verifier should flag as `equal` candidate. Confidence: high.
Depends on consequence relation. **Folksonomy signal** — near-duplicate of `positive_consequences`; cluster tag missing (null). Spec 4 verifier candidate. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument from Sign
rows.classifierNotes: Depends on sign-relation (X is a sign of Y; X observed → Y). Walton's classical sign scheme. Cluster tag missing — should be `evidence_family` or its own `sign_family`. Confidence: high.

rows.name: Argument Scheme Test
rows.classifierNotes: Test placeholder. **Folksonomy signal** — production catalogue contains a row literally named `test_scheme`. Should be deleted or moved to a `_test_fixtures` cluster. Confidence: high.

duplicateCandidates: near-synonymous; one has cluster tag, one does not
near-duplicate with directional refinement; one has cluster tag, one does not
